perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Person, other than its Representatives and in each case only those Representatives who need to receive such information in connection with its Evaluation and who have first been informe d of, and agreed to be bound by , the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluation Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Person, other than its Representatives, but the Recipient is not responsible or liable for the Representatives disclosure of Evaluation Material to any other person.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause by stating that the Recipient is not responsible for their Representatives' disclosure, whereas the original text clearly stipulates the Recipient's responsibility for any breach by its Representatives. Contradicting, because in section 3, it states “The Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives or by any other person to whom the Recipient ha s provided Evaluation Material…”, but the changed section states the Recipient isn’t responsible for that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will safeguard and strictly control the dissemination of the Evaluati on Material and not release or disclose any Evaluation Material to any Person, other than its Representatives and in each case only those Representatives who need to receive such information in connection with its Evaluation and who have first been informe d of, and agreed to be bound by , the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives or by any other person to whom the Recipient ha s provided Evaluation Material and shall, up on request, forthwith provide to the Disclosing Party a list of all parties to whom Evaluation Material has been provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Recipient is 'not responsible or liable for the Representatives disclosure of Evaluation Material to any other person.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states 'The Recipient agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives or by any other person to whom the Recipient ha s provided Evaluation Material'. The changed text removes the Recipient's responsibility for their representatives' actions, while the contradicted text explicitly states the Recipient is responsible. This creates a clear in-text contradiction, causing uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding responsibility for breaches of the agreement by representatives.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will and will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material; and (iii) provide to the Disclosing Party an officer ’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the Disclosing Party’s request , the Recipient will and will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party some of the Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, retaining copies or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); (ii) destroy or have destroyed some of the reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material; and (iii) provide to the Disclosing Party an officer’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity. The original text mandates returning *all* Evaluation Material and destroying *all* copies. The change states 'some of the evaluation Material', directly contradicting the original intention. The term being contradicted, is that in the original text, the Disclosing party receives all, but in the changed text, they are not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly upon the Disclosing Party ’s request , the Recipient will and will cause its Representatives to: (i) return to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material furnished to the Recipient or its Representatives, without retaining copie s or other reproductions, reports, extracts, notes or other memoranda thereof (whether electronic, magnetic or otherwise); (ii) destroy or have destroyed all reproductions, memoranda, notes, reports, extracts, compilations, analyses and documents and all d ocuments prepared by or in the possession of the Recipient or its Representatives related to the information contained in the Evaluation Material but which does not itself constitute Evaluation Material; and (iii) provide to the Disclosing Party an officer ’s certificate that the terms and conditions of this paragraph have been complied with.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by stating the recipient will return 'some' evaluation material and retain 'copies', directly contradicting the contradicted text which mandates returning 'all' evaluation material without retaining copies. The officer's certificate also states that the terms and conditions of the paragraph have been complied with, which is impossible if some material and copies are retained, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition applies here, as the altered clause, now in multiple locations, introduces conflict and uncertainty about the Recipient's obligation.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be gover ned by and construed in accordance with the laws of Alberta and b oth parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Alberta for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of the interp retation or enforcement of this Agreement (and both parties agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts) and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by personal delivery to its address set forth below shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against the Recipient in any such court.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be gover ned by and construed in accordance with the laws of Alberta, or any other location which the disclosing party may choose in it’s sole discretion, and b oth parties hereby irrevocably and conditionally consent to and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Alberta for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of the interp retation or enforcement of this Agreement (and both parties agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts) and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by personal delivery to its address set forth below shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against the Recipient in any such court.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies Alberta law as the governing law. The change adds 'or any other location which the disclosing party may choose in it’s sole discretion', introducing uncertainty about which jurisdiction applies. The 'conditionally consent' portion also creates contradiction and uncertianty with the original language, by removing the 'unconditionally' consent and adding 'conditionally', which directly goes against what it originally stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be gover ned by and construed in accordance with the laws of Alberta and b oth parties hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consent to and submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Alberta for any actions, suits or proceedings arising out of the interp retation or enforcement of this Agreement (and both parties agree not to commence any action, suit or proceeding relating thereto except in such courts) and further agree that service of any process, summons, notice or document by personal delivery to its address set forth below shall be effective service of process for any action, suit or proceeding brought against the Recipient in any such court.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text state that the agreement is governed by Alberta law. The changed text adds 'or any other location which the disclosing party may choose in it’s sole discretion,' creating a direct conflict. The original text states that both parties 'irrevocably and unconditionally consent' but the changed text modifies this to 'conditionally consent' creating another direct conflict. This change introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original agreement, making enforcement unclear as stated in the perturbation explanation, satisfying the definition of a structural flaw.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives are making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material and neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives or any of their respective officers, directors, employees or agents will have any liability whatsoever to the Recipient or to any other Person resulting from its use of the Evaluation Material and that the Recipient is and will be relying upon its own investigations, due diligence and analysis in evaluating and satisfying itself as to all matters relating to the Disclosing Party, its direct and indirect subsidiaries and their business, affairs and assets.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient understands and acknowledges that the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives are making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material and neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives or any of their respective officers, directors, employees or agents will have any liability whatsoever to the Recipient or to any other Person resulting from its use of the Evaluation Material and that the Recipient is and will be relying upon its own investigations, due diligence and analysis in evaluating and satisfying itself as to all matters relating to the Disclosing Party, its direct and indirect subsidiaries and their business, affairs and assets. However, the Disclosing Party warrants that they have taken all the necessary steps to ensure compliance with respect to this confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The initial part of the original clause disclaims any warranty regarding the accuracy of the Evaluation Material, this directly contradicts with 'However, the Disclosing Party warrants that they have taken all the necessary steps to ensure compliance with respect to this confidentiality agreement'. This creates confusion, as the reader would not know whether to believe that they don't have warranty on evaluation, or that they do.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient understands and acknowledges that neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives are making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Material and neither the Disclosing Party nor its Representatives or any of their respective officers, directors, employees or agents will have any liability whatsoever to the Recipient or to any other Person resulting from its use of the Evaluation Material and that the Recipient is and will be relying upon its own investigations, due diligence and analysis in evaluating and satisfying itself as to all matters relating to the Disclosing Party, its direct and indirect subsidiaries and their business, affairs and assets.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text are the same, which disclaims any warranty regarding the accuracy of the Evaluation Material. The changed text adds a clause that 'the Disclosing Party warrants that they have taken all the necessary steps to ensure compliance with respect to this confidentiality agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction. The Recipient is told they cannot rely on the Disclosing Party's accuracy or completeness, but then warranted that the Disclosing Party has taken all necessary steps to ensure compliance with respect to this confidentiality agreement. This contradicts the 'no representation or warranty' clause. As per the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition, 'Structural flaws also cause in-text contradictions when a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.' Here, a clause is placed in the same location as one that denies any warranty, but then gives a warranty. This causes a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party without the prior written consent of the other.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be assignable by either party without the prior written consent of the other, provided that the disclosing party agrees with the assignment.
perturbation.explanation: The original text indicates that this agreement is not assignable by either party without prior written consent. By adding the additional text “provided that the disclosing party agrees with the assignment” the reader is confused about whether or not the agreement can be assigned. The contradiction between whether the agreement can be assigned or not makes this an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not be assignable by either party without the prior written consent of the other.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is not assignable without prior written consent. The changed text modifies this to allow assignability without prior written consent if the disclosing party agrees, which directly contradicts the original restriction on assignment. The contradicted text is the exact same as the original text, reinforcing the contradiction introduced by the change.
